page.title: Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Sedmistřelná ikona]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Siedmiostrzelna”]][[ru:Семистрельная икона]][[uk:Семистрільна ікона Божої матері]][[uz:Xudoning onasining yetti oʻqli ikonasi]]




 

[[File:Семистрельныя икона с частицей by Nikolaj Velimirović.jpg|thumb]]
{{ill|ru:Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Семистрельная икона|qq=}}
{{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} is a Marian apparition<ref name=":0">Paraskeva Pyatnitsa / Levkievskaya E. E., Tolstaya S. M.  // Slavic Antiquities : Ethnolinguistic Dictionary: in 5 volumes  / under the general ed. N. I. Tolstoy ; Institute of Slavic Studies RAS . - <abbr>M .</abbr>  : Interd. relations , 2009. - V. 4: P (Crossing the water) - S (Sieve). - S. 631-633. - {{ISBN|5-7133-0703-4|978-5-7133-1312-8}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-09-15 |title=Seven Swords Pierced the Immaculate Heart of Mary |url=https://www.ncregister.com/blog/seven-swords-pierced-the-immaculate-heart-of-mary |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=NCR |language=en}}</ref>. She has seven swords sticking out of her chest.<ref name=":0" />

==Overview==

'''Semistrelnaya Icon of the Mother of God''' (Семистрельная икона Божией Матери) — a venerated {{ill|Russian Orthodox Church|en|Russian Orthodox Church|qid=Q60995|lt=icon|1=Russian Orthodox Church}} of the [[:en:Mother of God|Mother of God]]. The commemoration of the icon is held on August 26.

The original icon comes from the Toshnenskaya Church of St. John the Theologian in the vicinity of {{ill|Vologda|ja|Вологда|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vologda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, on the bank of the {{ill|Toshna River|ja|Тошня (река)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshna River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, the icon was found by a peasant from {{ill|Kadnikovsky Uyezd|ja|Кадниковский уезд|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadnikovsky Uyezd|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who discovered it in the {{ill|bell tower|ja|колокольня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell tower|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the church, where people had walked over it, mistaking it for a board. The icon became particularly famous in 1830 when a {{ill|Cholera|en|Cholera|qid=Q12090|lt=Cholera|1=Cholera}} epidemic ravaged Vologda. After 1917, the icon disappeared from the Church of St. John the Theologian<ref name="Vasilyeva">''Vasilyeva A. V.'' [http://www.portal-slovo.ru/art/41892.php Icon of the Mother of God 'Semistrelnaya' ('Softening of Evil Hearts')]. {{Wayback|url=http://www.portal-slovo.ru/art/41892.php |date=20150721024424 }}.</ref>.

Currently, the icon resides in the Church of St. Lazarus in Vologda. It has been in this church since 1945, after World War II<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://azbyka.ru/days/ikona-umjagchenie-zlyh-serdec-semistrelnaja |title=Icon of the Mother of God 'Softening of Evil Hearts' (Semistrelnaya). |access-date=2022-08-30 |archive-date=2022-08-30 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220830193312/https://azbyka.ru/days/ikona-umjagchenie-zlyh-serdec-semistrelnaja |deadlink=no }}</ref><ref>[https://www.booksite.ru/civk/album5-XVIII.html Image of the Mother of God 'Semistrelnaya'] {{Wayback|url=https://www.booksite.ru/civk/album5-XVIII.html |date=20220830193315 }}. Sorokin A. Lazarus Church: History of Our Piety // Blagovestnik. — 2005, № 7—9.</ref>.

The icon has an ancient origin (19th-century publications state that it is at least 500 years old<ref>''[[Poselyanin, Evgeny Nikolaevich|Poselyanin E. N.]]'' [http://azbyka.ru/fiction/bogomater-opisanie-ee-zemnoj-zhizni-i-chudotvornyx-ikon/#n22 The Mother of God: A Description of Her Earthly Life and Miraculous Icons]. {{Wayback|url=http://azbyka.ru/fiction/bogomater-opisanie-ee-zemnoj-zhizni-i-chudotvornyx-ikon/#n22 |date=20150505092624 }}.</ref>). The image was painted on a {{ill|canvas|ja|холст|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/canvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} glued to a board, dating it to the 18th century (possibly copied from an unknown 17th-century image)<ref name="Vasilyeva"/>. The Mother of God is depicted pierced by seven arrows.

The iconography is similar to the icon of the Mother of God {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ja|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or 'Simeon's Prophecy,' which has its features — three swords (arrows) are placed on both sides of the Mother of God and one below. Both icons are now considered variations of the same iconographic type<ref name="Vasilyeva"/>.

Believers pray before the image for reconciliation of enemies and deliverance from hard-heartedness<ref name="Vasilyeva"/>.

== Icons of the Mother of God with similar iconography ==
* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ja|Умягчение злых сердец|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or ''Simeon's Prophecy''
* {{ill|Passion Icon of the Mother of God#Passion Icon 'And a Sword Will Pierce Your Own Soul Too'|ja|Страстная икона Божией Матери#Страстная икона «И тебе самой душу пройдёт оружие»|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Passion Icon of the Mother of God#Passion Icon 'And a Sword Will Pierce Your Own Soul Too'|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Literature ==
* {{книга
|author = {{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ja|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
|title = Detailed Menologion of Saints Shone in the Russian Land. To Which Icons Should One Pray for Various Needs
|edition = Facsimile edition
|place = St. Petersburg
|publisher = Vera
|year = 1997
|pages = 405
}}
